name: Home security systems
summary: DIY and professionally-monitored home security systems, $200-$2000 hardware plus $10-$60/month monitoring.

evidence.claim: UL 827 is the US Standard for Central-Station Alarm Services; professionally monitored systems must route alarms through a UL-listed central station. Non-UL services can still dispatch police but are not insurance-rated.
FCC Z-Wave / Zigbee interoperability documentation — Z-Wave 700/800 series and Zigbee 3.0 are required for vendor-independent sensor pairing.
CISA 2024 Home Security IoT guidance warning on default-password devices and lack of firmware update commitments in low-end Chinese-OEM IP cameras.

criteria.name: ul_listed_central_station
criteria.notes: UL 827 central-station listing is required for most insurance discounts (E1).

criteria.name: cellular_backup
criteria.notes: Cellular/LTE backup keeps system armed during internet/power outages.

criteria.name: security_record
criteria.notes: CVE count, default-password posture, firmware update commitment (E3).

confabulationPatterns.reality: Often bundled with 36-60 month monitoring contracts that cost $2000+ total; 'free' hardware often carries cancellation fees >$500. Cancellation window is typically 3 days post-install.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: What's the contract length, monthly cost, and cancellation fee?

confabulationPatterns.reality: No defined standard. Meaningful security posture is AES-128+ at rest, TLS 1.2+ in transit, and a documented CVE disclosure process (E3). 'Military-grade' is marketing copy.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Does the vendor publish a CVE disclosure policy and firmware update commitment period?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Computer-vision detection accuracy varies 60-95% across brands and is highly lighting-dependent. False-positive rates of 5-15/week are common for lower-tier cameras.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the detection rate cited with a false-positive figure and independent testing source?

counterfeitSignals.signal: Ring/Nest/Arlo cameras from non-authorized Amazon seller at 40%+ below MSRP
counterfeitSignals.action: flag — commonly grey-market imports without US firmware-update support

compatibilityQuestions: Renting or owning? Renters need wireless DIY systems with no drilling.
Existing smart-home ecosystem (Alexa, HomeKit, Google)? Matter-compatible systems integrate best.
Required by homeowner's insurance for discount? May need UL 827 central-station monitoring.

typicalHiddenCosts.name: Permit fee (many municipalities require alarm permits)

repairability.notes: Open-standard (Z-Wave/Zigbee/Matter) systems support third-party replacement sensors. Proprietary hubs (Ring, SimpliSafe) require OEM sensors.

representativeSkus.name: iota Security Kit
representativeSkus.notes: Z-Wave + Zigbee + HomeKit + Alexa — best open-interop.

representativeSkus.name: Smart Hub + Door/Window Sensors
representativeSkus.notes: 5-year contract; aggressive door-to-door sales channel + high cancellation fees.

representativeSkus.name: 5-Piece Home Alarm Kit
representativeSkus.notes: No professional monitoring option; insurance discount typically unavailable.
